page.content: In the second quarter of fiscal year 2025, Rio Tinto reported significant financial metrics with total Revenue of $450M, Net Income of $120M, EBITDA of $200M, a Gross Margin of 30%, an Operating Margin of 27%, CAPEX of $50M, Operating Expenses of $180M, and a Net Profit Margin of 26.7% with a Production Volume of 150,000 tons. For Q1 FY2025, the figures were Revenue at $400M, Net Income at $100M, and EBITDA at $180M, whereas Year-to-Date totals reached $850M in Revenue, $220M in Net Income, and $370M in EBITDA, reflecting a Revenue Growth of 12.5%.

Income of $120M, EBITDA of $200M, a Gross Margin of 30%, an Operating Margin of 27%, CAPEX of $50M, Operating

page.content: In the exploration metrics, Project A was drilled for 5,000 meters with a Success Rate of 75%, incurring a CAPEX of $1.5M, OPEX of $300K, and yielding a Net Income of $600K, with Total Assets of $600K.

lines.text: In the exploration metrics, Project A was drilled for 5,000 meters with a Success Rate of 75%, incurring a CAPEX of $1.5M,
OPEX of $300K, and yielding a Net Income of $600K, with Total Assets of $600K.

page.content: Meanwhile, Project B reported 6,200 meters drilled, an 80% Success Rate, a CAPEX of $2.0M, OPEX of $400K, and a Net Income of $800K, with Total Liabilities of $800K. Project C had 4,500 meters drilled and a Success Rate of 70%, with a CAPEX of $1.2M, OPEX of $250K, a Net Income of $500K, and Equity of $500K, while Project D was drilled for 3,800 meters with a 65% Success Rate, incurring a CAPEX of $900K, OPEX of $200K, and a Net Income of $400K, with Total assets of $400K.
